permanently_banned.reason: Cardiovascular risks including heart attack and stroke
permanently_banned.notes: First major supplement ban by FDA. Still found in some imported products.

permanently_banned.reason: Cardiovascular risks and false marketing claims
permanently_banned.notes: Often disguised as 'geranium extract' despite not being naturally occurring in geranium.

permanently_banned.reason: Severe cardiotoxicity, substituted in tejocote root supplements
permanently_banned.notes: Multiple Class I recalls in 2024-2025. Pattern shows deliberate substitution.
Marketed as DMAA replacement. Found in pre-workout supplements.

permanently_banned.reason: Opioid-like effects, addiction potential, overdose risk
permanently_banned.notes: Schedule I in Alabama, Utah. FDA issued multiple warnings 2024-2025. Linked to hospitalizations and deaths.
Schedule II in Alabama, Schedule I in Utah. Can cause life-threatening withdrawal.
Often falsely claimed to be from Acacia rigidula. Added to WADA 2024 prohibited list.

permanently_banned.reason: Unapproved drug, does not meet supplement definition
permanently_banned.notes: Synthetic compound developed in Russia as pharmaceutical drug.
Recent FDA warning letters July 2025. Found in concentrated kratom products. FDA recommending Schedule I classification.

permanently_banned.reason: Increased risk of heart attack and stroke
permanently_banned.notes: Most commonly found adulterant in weight loss supplements (28% of tested products).

permanently_banned.reason: Thyroid cancer in animal studies (Delaney Clause)
permanently_banned.notes: Effective date Jan 15, 2027 for foods/supplements, Jan 18, 2028 for ingested drugs. Banned in cosmetics since 1990.
Found substituted in tejocote root supplements. Causes neurologic, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular toxicity. Class 1 recalls issued.

permanently_banned.reason: Unapproved SYNTHETIC drug ingredient, growth hormone-related compound, safety concerns
permanently_banned.notes: SYNTHETIC growth hormone-related compounds banned in dietary supplements. Natural IGF-1 from deer antler velvet remains legal. Check source and synthetic vs natural distinction.

permanently_banned.reason: Drug ingredient exclusion rule - CBD is an approved drug ingredient (Epidiolex)
permanently_banned.notes: CBD cannot be marketed in dietary supplements under US FDA rules due to drug exclusion rule. Legal in some states for other uses.

permanently_banned.reason: Beta-2 agonist with cardiovascular risks, WADA prohibited
permanently_banned.notes: Found in pre-workout and weight loss supplements. Can cause dangerous arrhythmias.

permanently_banned.reason: Flame retardant chemical with potential thyroid and reproductive health risks
permanently_banned.notes: FDA banned effective July 2024. Flame retardant chemical used as emulsifier in citrus-flavored drinks. Linked to thyroid dysfunction and reproductive issues in animal studies.

nootropic_banned.notes: Found in supplements despite being unapproved drug. Only levetiracetam approved in US.
WADA prohibited as stimulant. Not approved as supplement ingredient.
Discontinued in France 2011. Found contaminating piracetam supplements. DoD prohibited.
Schedule IV controlled substance. Found in nootropic supplements.
FDA warning letter April 2025 to Gluten Free Remedies LLC.
Found in all 10 tested nootropic supplements. Russian pharmaceutical.
Approved for dementia in Italy/Argentina/China. Found undeclared in supplements.
FDA warning for women of childbearing age. Pharmaceutical in Germany/Russia/China.
Russian pharmaceutical. Found in nootropic supplements 2025.
Found in cognitive enhancement supplements. No human safety data.
Found in nootropic supplements 2025. More potent than modafinil.

nootropic_banned.reason: Unapproved new drug; not GRAS; requires IND for human use
nootropic_banned.notes: Synthetic nootropic. Not approved for sale as dietary supplement in the US.

nootropic_banned.reason: Unapproved new drug; failed clinical trials; not approved for supplements
nootropic_banned.notes: Experimental drug. Not approved for dietary supplements.

nootropic_banned.reason: Unapproved synthetic compound; not approved for human consumption
nootropic_banned.notes: Experimental cognitive enhancer. Never approved for any use.

sarms_prohibited.notes: Most common SARM found in supplements. Linked to liver toxicity.
Frequently found in bodybuilding supplements. Can cause testosterone suppression.
Not technically a SARM but grouped with them. Caused cancer in multiple animal studies.
Can cause yellow-tinted vision and night blindness.
Marketed as testosterone replacement alternative. Reports of liver damage.

sarms_prohibited.reason: Unapproved SARM; synthetic anabolic compound; not approved for human use
sarms_prohibited.notes: Myostatin inhibitor marketed as SARM. Banned from supplements.

sarms_prohibited.reason: Unapproved SARM; potential male contraceptive; not approved for supplements
sarms_prohibited.notes: One of the most potent SARMs. Completely banned from supplements.

sarms_prohibited.reason: Unapproved synthetic compound; Rev-ErbA agonist; not approved for human use
sarms_prohibited.notes: Marketed as exercise mimetic. Not a true SARM but banned similarly.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Anxiety, heart palpitations, dangerous blood pressure changes
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Can interact dangerously with antidepressants and blood pressure medications.
Often used as ephedra replacement. Can cause similar cardiovascular issues.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Severe liver damage, banned in multiple countries
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Over 100 cases of severe liver toxicity reported. Banned in many countries.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Liver damage, cancer risk from pyrrolizidine alkaloids
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Contains hepatotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Banned for internal use in many countries.
Class 1 carcinogen. Causes irreversible kidney damage and cancer.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Liver damage at doses >800mg EGCG daily
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Safe at normal doses. EFSA set upper limit at 800mg EGCG per day.
Multiple cases of kidney failure. Subject to import alerts from Korea.
Not lawfully marketed as supplement. FDA warnings ongoing. Deaths reported with combination use.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Metal fibers can cause serious injury to digestive system, including perforation
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Major recalls in Canada affecting 22+ supplement brands including multivitamins, prenatal vitamins, and specialized supplements. Metal fibers originated from manufacturing equipment malfunction.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Lack of sterility assurance, potential bacterial contamination in compounded formulations
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Multiple Class II recalls for contaminated compounding pharmacy GLP-1 products. Risk of serious infections from non-sterile preparations. FDA warning against non-FDA approved compounded versions.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Potential harm to children's health, reassessment of risk-benefit profile
high_risk_ingredients.notes: FDA announced plans to remove fluoride supplements for children from market following safety review. Concerns about fluorosis and other developmental effects. Still under regulatory review.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Severe hepatotoxicity; can cause liver failure and death even in small doses
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Especially dangerous when used to induce abortion. Multiple deaths reported.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Severe liver damage; linked to hepatitis and liver failure
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Associated with at least 18 cases of liver toxicity. FDA warning since 1992.

high_risk_ingredients.reason: Hepatotoxicity; liver damage; multiple cases of acute hepatitis
high_risk_ingredients.notes: Banned in France and Germany. FDA warned against use in weight loss products.

illegal_spiking_agents.notes: Most common adulterant in male enhancement supplements. Can be fatal with heart medications.

illegal_spiking_agents.reason: Designer drug analog of banned tadalafil
illegal_spiking_agents.notes: Found in Health Fixer products (04/29/2025). Emerging designer analog.

illegal_spiking_agents.reason: Designer drug analog of banned sildenafil
illegal_spiking_agents.notes: Found in Health Fixer (04/29/2025). Designer analog to evade bans.
Second most common adulterant in sexual enhancement products.

illegal_spiking_agents.reason: Banned weight loss drug with cardiovascular risks
illegal_spiking_agents.notes: Found in 28% of tested weight loss supplements. Banned since 2010.
Removed from OTC laxatives in 1999 due to cancer risk. Still found in weight loss products.
Found in 21% of tested weight loss supplements. Can cause dangerous interactions.
Found in 26% of tested bodybuilding supplements. Often unlisted designer steroids.
Found in multiple recalled herbal supplements 2024.
Found adulterating kratom products. Caused hemorrhagic stroke case. WADA prohibited 2015.
New synthetic derivative, 10-20x more potent than natural kratom alkaloids. Multiple deaths reported 2025.

illegal_spiking_agents.reason: Designer drug variants of banned tianeptine
illegal_spiking_agents.notes: New designer variants emerging to circumvent bans. Multiple hospitalizations 2025.

heavy_metal_limits.notes: California Prop 65 limit much stricter. Common in herbal supplements from certain regions. Indian supplements show higher risk.
heavy_metal_limits.reason: Neurotoxic; harmful to brain development and cognitive function. Prop 65 limit: 0.5 mcg/day

heavy_metal_limits.notes: Inorganic arsenic more toxic than organic forms. Common in rice-based products. Recalled in Zaarah Herbals products 2025.
heavy_metal_limits.reason: Carcinogenic; linked to skin, lung, and bladder cancer. FDA concern level varies by product type

heavy_metal_limits.health_effects: Kidney damage, bone disease, lung damage
heavy_metal_limits.notes: Accumulates in kidneys. Higher levels in chocolate and zinc supplements.
heavy_metal_limits.reason: Kidney damage; bone loss. California Prop 65 limit: 4.1 mcg/day

heavy_metal_limits.notes: Methylmercury most toxic form. Fish oil should be molecularly distilled. High levels in some TCM products.
heavy_metal_limits.reason: Neurotoxic; harmful to developing fetuses and children. EPA reference dose: 0.1 mcg/kg/day

wada_prohibited_2024.notes: New addition to 2024 WADA list. 24-hour washout period required.
wada_prohibited_2024.reason: Prohibited by World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) for athletic competitions

wada_prohibited_2024.notes: In-competition only. CBD not prohibited if THC <0.3%.
wada_prohibited_2024.reason: Prohibited by World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) for athletic competitions

state_regional_bans.notes: Louisiana Schedule I effective Aug 1, 2025. Federal scheduling under consideration.
State-level bans expanding beyond federal action. Includes all analogs.

manufacturing_violations.reason: Carcinogenic impurities from manufacturing processes, DNA-damaging compounds
manufacturing_violations.notes: Carcinogenic manufacturing impurities found in supplements similar to pharmaceutical contamination. NDMA and NDEA are probable human carcinogens. Requires comprehensive testing and manufacturing controls.

new_emerging_threats.notes: Emerging threat of AI-designed compounds with unknown safety profiles.
Yellow oleander in tejocote root. Expanding FDA investigations. Class 1 recalls.

new_emerging_threats.reason: Unknown safety profile, potential endocrine disruption
new_emerging_threats.notes: Increasing presence in bodybuilding supplements. No safety data.
Multiple recalls 2025 for toxic mushroom contamination in supplements.

new_emerging_threats.reason: Synthetic compounds sold as natural bacopa extract
new_emerging_threats.notes: Investigation found synthetic compounds in 'natural' bacopa products 2025.

pharmaceutical_adulterants.notes: Found in 15% of tested male enhancement products 2025.
Withdrawn from market 2020, still found in supplements 2025.

pharmaceutical_adulterants.reason: Severe psychiatric side effects including depression, anxiety, and suicidal ideation
pharmaceutical_adulterants.notes: Never approved in US; withdrawn from EU market 2008. Still found in weight loss supplements.

pharmaceutical_adulterants.reason: Prescription diabetes medication illegally added to weight loss supplements
pharmaceutical_adulterants.notes: Can cause dangerous hypoglycemia. Found in numerous recalled weight loss products.

designer_stimulants.notes: Found in pre-workout supplements labeled as 'proprietary blend'.
New analogs designed to evade DMAA/DMHA bans.

synthetic_cannabinoids.notes: Found in products marketed as legal CBD alternatives.

novel_peptides.notes: Commonly found in oral supplements despite no oral bioavailability.

research_chemicals.notes: New derivatives emerging to evade WADA/FDA bans.

recalled_supplement_brands.reason: Linked to liver damage, jaundice, and one death. Multiple recalls.
recalled_supplement_brands.notes: Recalled in 2009. Reformulated but still associated with adverse events.

recalled_supplement_brands.reason: Acute liver failure; dozens of hospitalizations; one death; one liver transplant
recalled_supplement_brands.notes: Linked to outbreak of acute non-viral hepatitis in Hawaii. Permanently recalled.

recalled_supplement_brands.reason: Contained DMAA; linked to heart attacks and deaths in military personnel
recalled_supplement_brands.notes: Original formula banned. Linked to deaths of two US soldiers. Reformulated without DMAA.

_metadata.description: Banned and recalled ingredients database including FDA warnings, prohibited substances, and emerging threats
